_source: IDEO Design Thinking, TRIZ, Alex Osborn, Edward de Bono, Steve Jobs interviews
_note: Long-tail creativity data. Specific frameworks with actionable prompts and examples.

scamper.overview: Structured ideation checklist; each letter = transformation type

questions: What materials, parts, rules, or processes can be substituted?
What other ingredient can be used?
substitute.example_product: Replace plastic packaging with mushroom mycelium (Ecovative Design)
substitute.example_service: Substitute in-person therapy with AI therapist (Woebot)

questions: What ideas or materials can be combined?
combine.innovation_type: Most common source of breakthrough products

questions: What else is like this? What other context uses this approach?
What could be copied or adapted?
adapt.example: Adapt Velcro from burr seeds (George de Mestral)
adapt.cross_domain: Best innovations often adapt solutions from completely different fields

questions: What to make larger, stronger, longer, more frequent?
examples: Magnify: Grand Theft Auto open world scale

questions: What else can this be used for?
Who else could use this product?
examples: WD-40 designed for rockets; now consumer lubricant
Post-it Notes: failed adhesive turned office staple

questions: What if I removed a core component?
examples: Remove keyboard from phone = iPhone revolution
Remove TV from TV subscription = Netflix streaming
eliminate.principle: Subtraction is often more powerful than addition

questions: What if I did the opposite?
What if I reversed the roles?
examples: Reverse: instead of customer going to store, store comes to customer (Uber Eats)
Rearrange: Airbnb — make guests the product, hosts the supply

empathize.key_insight: People can't articulate what they want; observe what they DO, not what they say

define.how_might_we: Reframe problem as opportunity: 'HMW help seniors feel connected?'

techniques: Reverse brainstorming (how do we make problem worse?)

prototype.fidelity_rule: Start lowest fidelity possible; paper prototype before Figma; Figma before code
prototype.time_box: 8-hour prototype rule; if you can't test a concept in a day, rethink it

test.5_user_rule: Nielsen Norman: 5 users find 85% of usability problems; diminishing returns after
test.think_aloud: Ask users to verbalize thoughts while using; most revealing feedback method

triz.overview: Theory of Inventive Problem Solving (Russian: ТРИЗ); 40 inventive principles

key_principles.name: Local quality
key_principles.description: Different parts of object have different functions

key_principles.name: Dynamics
key_principles.description: Make characteristics optimal at each stage

key_principles.name: Blessing in disguise
key_principles.description: Use harmful factors to get positive effect
key_principles.example: Noise-canceling uses ambient sound to cancel it

triz.contradiction_matrix: 40 principles mapped to 39 engineering parameters; find which principles resolve your specific contradiction

jobs_to_be_done.origin: Clayton Christensen, Harvard; 'customers hire products to do a job'
jobs_to_be_done.question: What job is the customer hiring this product/service to do?
jobs_to_be_done.milkshake_example: McDonald's milkshake wasn't competing with other milkshakes; it was hired for morning commute boredom
jobs_to_be_done.application: Interview customers: 'What were you doing before you discovered this product? What was frustrating?'
jobs_to_be_done.key_insight: Circumstances matter more than customer demographics; focus on the job, not the customer profile

hats.white: Facts and data only; what do we know?
hats.red: Emotions and intuition; how do you feel about this?
hats.black: Critical judgment; what could go wrong?
hats.blue: Process management; control the thinking process

six_thinking_hats.usage: Separate different types of thinking; everyone wears same hat simultaneously

pain_point_discovery.reddit_mining: Search '[topic] + sucks', '[topic] + frustrated', 'wish there was'; find unmet needs
pain_point_discovery.amazon_review_mining: 1-star reviews reveal pain points; 5-star reveals delight factors
pain_point_discovery.twitter_complaint_search: 'I hate [product]' OR 'why can't [product]' for specific frustrations

market_validation.presell_rule: Sell before you build; landing page + waitlist + stripe payment = real demand test
market_validation.mkbhd_rule: If you can't explain product in one sentence, it's not ready
market_validation.landing_page_metrics: 5%+ conversion on paid traffic = strong signal; <2% = rethink

innovation_types.sustaining: Improving existing product for existing customers; incremental
innovation_types.disruptive: Start worse but cheaper/accessible; improve until replacing incumbent (Christensen)
innovation_types.blue_ocean: Create uncontested new market space; not compete in red ocean (W. Chan Kim)

cross_domain_inspiration.principle: Breakthroughs often come from applying solutions from one domain to another

examples.innovation: Eastgate Centre Zimbabwe — passive cooling system
Atul Gawande applied aviation checklists to surgery; saved thousands of lives

cross_domain_inspiration.technique: Take your problem. Pick unrelated field (nature, military, cooking, sports). Find how THEY solve analogous problems. Map back.
